name: George Meany
abstract: William George Meany (August 16, 1894 – January 10, 1980) was an American labor union administrator for 57 years. He was a vital figure in the creation of the AFL–CIO and served as its first president, from 1955 to 1979.

concerts.label: Amalgamated Ladies' Garment Cutters' Union Local 10 Fiftieth Anniversary Program

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses George Meany's political and labor union stance ('represent the working class of America', 'alliance with the capitalists') but provides no evidence of a personal, professional, or musical connection.
